author: Priya Mehta
objective: Classify incoming support tickets by urgency and route them to the correct specialist queue within 30 seconds of receipt
input_signal: New ticket payload from Zendesk webhook — includes subject, body, customer tier, and sentiment score
evaluation_criteria: Tickets are scored on three axes: customer impact (revenue tier × active users), technical severity (service degradation level), and sentiment polarity. A composite score determines the routing path.

decision_nodes.label: Service outage detected
decision_nodes.condition: body contains keywords 'down', 'outage', '500 error' AND customer tier ∈ {enterprise, growth}
decision_nodes.description: Check whether the ticket describes a full or partial service outage for a high-value account
decision_nodes.outcome_true: Route to P0 incident queue and page on-call SRE immediately
decision_nodes.data_source: Zendesk ticket payload + PagerDuty active incidents feed

decision_nodes.label: Billing dispute flagged
decision_nodes.description: Detect billing-related complaints with negative sentiment that need urgent finance team review
decision_nodes.outcome_true: Route to billing-escalations queue with churn-risk tag
decision_nodes.data_source: Ticket subject line + Stripe payment history for customer

decision_nodes.label: Feature request identified
decision_nodes.condition: body matches 'would be nice|feature request|can you add|wish there was' AND sentiment_score > 0.1
decision_nodes.description: Identify constructive feature requests that should be logged in the product backlog rather than handled as support
decision_nodes.outcome_true: Auto-tag as feature-request, log to Productboard, send acknowledgment template

decision_nodes.label: Integration configuration issue
decision_nodes.condition: body references API|webhook|OAuth|integration AND customer has active API keys
decision_nodes.description: Detect technical integration issues that require developer-level support
decision_nodes.outcome_true: Route to integrations-specialist queue with customer's API usage summary attached
decision_nodes.data_source: Ticket body + internal API key registry

fallback_action: Route to general-support queue with normal priority and auto-assign to next available agent
fallback_trigger: No decision node exceeds the 0.85 confidence threshold or all conditions evaluate to false
fallback_notification: Post summary to #support-triage Slack channel for human review within 2 hours
include_reasoning: Yes — full decision path with per-node confidence scores and matched keywords
